title: Phlomoides ornata
taxonomy: Phlomoides ornata is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Lamiaceae, commonly known as the mint family. It belongs to the genus Phlomoides, which consists of several species of shrubby plants. The plant is native to Australia and is often found growing in dry, rocky areas. The classification of Phlomoides ornata is further supported by its characteristics, such as its hairy stems and leaves, and its small, white flowers. The specific classification of Phlomoides ornata is Phlomoides ornata (Benth.) G.Tucker.
